data.body: PREPLANNING_CONTEXT: {"version":1,"epicId":"16f8-4b57","generatedAt":"2026-04-13T18:22:51.906400+00:00","generatedBy":"preplanning","epic":{"title":"Worktree/Branch Session Tracking via Ticket Comments","description":"When implementation sub-agents and sprint/fix-bug orchestrators begin work, there is no record of which branch or worktree holds their in-progress changes. Ticket comments track checkpoint progress via CHECKPOINT:phase-name prefixes but not branch location. Tracking and recovery must ship together.","successCriteria":["When an implementation sub-agent begins work on a task ticket, it posts a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment recording its branch name and the orchestrator's session branch before any implementation begins.","When sprint or fix-bug begins a session on an epic or bug ticket, it posts a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment recording the session branch before dispatching sub-agents.","When work completes via harvest-worktree.sh (on any exit path \u2014 success or failure), a WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete comment is written with the branch name and outcome (merged or discarded).","When resuming a ticket with an unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment (no corresponding complete), the orchestrator autonomously assesses the abandoned branch: merges it into the current session branch if it contains at least one commit not reachable from the session branch HEAD and the merge produces no conflicts; discards it if already merged (no unique commits) or if any merge conflict requires manual resolution.","When two competing unmerged implementations exist on a ticket, the orchestrator selects the better one using this priority order: (1) most AC items addressed; (2) test-gate-status artifact; (3) fewest merge conflicts; (4) most recently started by timestamp.","When a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start references a branch that no longer exists, the orchestrator skips it without error and proceeds.","An integration test fixture stages an interrupted-session state and verifies that resuming the ticket results in an autonomous merge or discard decision without any manual git commands.","After a story's or bug's changes are merged to main, a WORKTREE_TRACKING:landed comment is written on that ticket confirming the merge completed.","When resuming, the orchestrator scans for WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments on both the top-level epic/bug ticket AND all child story and task tickets.","When ticket comment writes fail, WORKTREE_TRACKING writes fail silently without blocking sub-agent work; resume skips tracking-based assessment and falls back to checkpoint-only behavior.","When resuming a ticket with no WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment, the orchestrator proceeds with existing checkpoint-only resume behavior unchanged."]},"stories":[{"id":"06da-ab37","title":"As a developer, sprint and sub-agents record their active branch on each ticket via WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments","description":"Adds WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment writes to sub-agent dispatch and orchestrator session start, plus defines the comment format spec.","priority":2,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":true,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["A WORKTREE_TRACKING comment format specification is defined (covering :start, :complete, and :landed \u2014 field names, serialization, example for each) and referenced by Stories B and C","Any implementation sub-agent writes a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment on its task ticket before beginning implementation, failing silently if .tickets-tracker/ is unavailable","Sprint writes :start on the epic ticket in Phase 1 after ticket validation; fix-bug writes :start on the bug ticket in Step 0.5 after ticket is set to in_progress","Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic"],"considerations":["[Implementation] Sub-agent :start writes require modifying task-execution.md template to inject write before CHECKPOINT 1/6","[Maintainability] :start writes span sprint SKILL.md, fix-bug SKILL.md, and task-execution.md \u2014 format spec prevents drift","[Reliability] :start writes must fail silently when .tickets-tracker/ is detached or read-only"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":[],"satisfiesCriterion":"When an implementation sub-agent begins work on a task ticket, it posts a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment recording its branch name and the orchestrator's session branch before any implementation begins."},{"id":"d2ef-92cc","title":"As a developer, worktree harvesting and story/bug merges record their outcome via WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete and :landed comments","description":"Modifies harvest-worktree.sh and per-worktree-review-commit.md for :complete writes; adds :landed writes to the session-close flow.","priority":2,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":true,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["harvest-worktree.sh writes WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete on every exit path with outcome=merged|discarded; per-worktree-review-commit.md updated to supply ticket ID and write :complete on its failure path","After story/bug merges to main, WORKTREE_TRACKING:landed written on each affected ticket in session-close flow","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Implementation] :complete logic location (inside harvest vs. orchestrator) is an implementation decision; per-worktree-review-commit.md must be in scope either way","[Reliability] harvest-worktree.sh ERR/EXIT trap must handle missing ticket ID gracefully (very early exit)","[Implementation] :landed write occurs in end-session/SKILL.md Step 4 post-merge, not in merge-to-main.sh itself"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["06da-ab37"],"satisfiesCriterion":"When work completes via harvest-worktree.sh (on any exit path \u2014 success or failure), a WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete comment is written with the branch name and outcome (merged or discarded)."},{"id":"930f-8991","title":"As a developer, resuming a ticket automatically detects and recovers a single abandoned worktree branch","description":"Adds resume-time scanning logic to sprint and fix-bug to detect unmatched :start comments and autonomously merge or discard each abandoned branch.","priority":2,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":true,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["Sprint and fix-bug scan for unmatched :start comments at session start (via parent_id ticket enumeration) and autonomously merge (unique commits + no conflicts) or discard each abandoned branch; git merge --abort on mid-merge interruption","Non-existent branch references skipped without error","Branches already merged into HEAD treated as already_merged with retroactive :complete comment","Dual-level scan covers top-level ticket AND all children (open and closed)","No :start comment = checkpoint-only resume behavior unchanged","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Implementation] Child enumeration via ticket list + parent_id filtering (two levels), NOT ticket deps","[Implementation] Sprint: Auto-Resume Detection section; fix-bug: add after Step 0.5","[Reliability] git merge --abort cleanup on mid-merge interruption","[Reliability] already-ancestor branch = already_merged, no re-merge, write retroactive :complete","[Performance] Skip tickets with no :start comment to limit CLI calls","[Scope] debug-everything gap: document as follow-up"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":"foundation","splitPairId":"7de2-f557","blockedBy":["06da-ab37","d2ef-92cc"],"satisfiesCriterion":"When resuming a ticket with an unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment (no corresponding complete), the orchestrator autonomously assesses the abandoned branch."},{"id":"7de2-f557","title":"As a developer, when multiple competing branches exist for a ticket, the system automatically selects the best implementation","description":"Extends resume logic to tiebreak between multiple unmatched :start branches using AC count \u2192 test-status \u2192 conflict-count \u2192 timestamp cascade.","priority":3,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["Tiebreak cascade applied when multiple unmatched :start comments from distinct branches exist on the same ticket; winning branch merged, losers discarded","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Testing] Edge cases: no task-list items, identical AC counts, missing test-gate-status, all tiebreakers tied","[Maintainability] Encode as shared helper function with labeled tiebreak stages"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":"enhancement","splitPairId":"930f-8991","blockedBy":["930f-8991"],"satisfiesCriterion":"When two competing unmerged implementations exist on a ticket, the orchestrator selects the better one using this priority order."},{"id":"d187-aa91","title":"As a developer, an integration test fixture verifies autonomous recovery from an interrupted-session state","description":"Creates integration test in tests/ using real temp-dir git repo to verify end-to-end resume recovery.","priority":2,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["Integration test in tests/ stages interrupted-session state with unmatched :start comment and branch with unmerged commits; asserts autonomous merge or discard without manual git commands","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Testing] Use real git init in temp dir, not mocked git commands","[Reliability] Deterministic: fixed branch names, commit messages, timestamp seeds","[Testing] Cover both merge path and discard path as separate test cases"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["06da-ab37","d2ef-92cc","930f-8991"],"satisfiesCriterion":"An integration test fixture stages an interrupted-session state and verifies that resuming the ticket results in an autonomous merge or discard decision without any manual git commands."},{"id":"991c-1f9d","title":"Update project docs to reflect WORKTREE_TRACKING comment lifecycle","description":"Updates CLAUDE.md and other existing docs to describe the tracking comment lifecycle and resume logic.","priority":3,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["CLAUDE.md accurately describes WORKTREE_TRACKING:start/complete/landed lifecycle, autonomous resume logic, and debug-everything gap as known limitation"],"considerations":["Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["06da-ab37","d2ef-92cc","930f-8991","7de2-f557"],"satisfiesCriterion":"Documentation reflects the full set of tracking comment SCs delivered by Stories A-D"}],"storyDashboard":{"totalStories":6,"uiStories":0,"criticalPath":["06da-ab37","d2ef-92cc","930f-8991","7de2-f557","d187-aa91","991c-1f9d"]}}
